source_record_id: 2023 - Unsupervised Deep Learning of Sparse Signals against Low-Rank Backgrounds with Application to Online.pdf
file_name: 2023 - Unsupervised Deep Learning of Sparse Signals against Low-Rank Backgrounds with Application to Online.pdf
title: Unsupervised Deep Learning of Sparse Signals against Low-Rank Backgrounds with Application to Online Lung Sound Separation
authors: Takumi Onomichi; Tomoya Sakai; Yasushi Obase
venue_source: International Journal of Signal Processing Systems
abstract_summary: Proposes unsupervised deep learning for sparse signal extraction from low-rank backgrounds and applies it to online lung sound separation from auscultation spectrograms.
keywords: nuclear loss; dual frame U-Net; low-rank and sparse model; lung sound analysis
method_model: Dual-frame U-Net trained with nuclear norm and l1 sparse loss, inspired by low-rank plus sparse modeling/RPCA.
dataset_used: Auscultatory lung sounds with wheezes and rhonchi; exact public availability not clear from local PDF text.
evaluation_metrics: Qualitative spectrogram comparison and RPCA comparison; runtime/online separation discussion.
key_contribution: Shows an unsupervised U-Net-style sparse extraction approach that can separate structured lung sound components faster than iterative RPCA.
limitation: Focuses on lung adventitious sound extraction rather than heart-lung cardiopulmonary separation; dataset details and standard source separation metrics are limited.
local_pdf_path: papers\pdfs\2023 - Unsupervised Deep Learning of Sparse Signals against Low-Rank Backgrounds with Application to Online.pdf

method_model: LU-Net deep encoder-decoder denoising architecture for PCG noise suppression.
dataset_used: Benchmark PCG dataset mixed with breathing/lung sounds and hospital noise; PASCAL heart sound dataset noisy portion.
evaluation_metrics: SNR improvement; classification accuracy improvement on noisy PASCAL subset; parameter count and real-time performance.
key_contribution: Useful denoising evidence for suppressing lung/breathing interference from heart sounds and evaluating SNR improvement.
limitation: Primary goal is heart sound denoising and cardiac disease detection, not separation into both heart and lung outputs.
